consistency.general: The item describes a base functionality for all Ioun Stones (orbiting head, AC/HP for disruption) and then lists specific properties based on shape, color, and rarity. The structure is consistent across the variants.
consistency.mechanics: Mechanics for each variant are clearly described (stat increases, spell absorption limits, regeneration timing, spell storage rules). The general rules for removal/destruction are consistent.
consistency.formatting: The formatting uses bolding and italicization effectively to distinguish the stone names and effects. There is a slight formatting inconsistency where the general description mentions '1d3 feet' for distance, but this is standard flavor text for the base item.

quality.completeness: The entry is comprehensive, covering activation, removal, object stats, and specific magical effects for numerous variants.
quality.clarity: The descriptions are generally very clear. The rules for spell absorption (tracking levels spent, burnout condition) and spell reserve (slot level usage, spellcasting ability retention) are well-defined.
quality.flavor: The descriptions effectively use shape and color to differentiate the stones, enhancing flavor.

mechanics_evaluation.base_item: Standard and balanced. AC 24 / 10 HP with resistance makes it hard to remove non-magically, fitting for a high-tier magic item.
mechanics_evaluation.stat_increase_stones: Standard bonuses (+2 to score, max 20) for Very Rare items.
mechanics_evaluation.absorption_stones: Rarity scaling (Very Rare vs. Legendary) is clearly tied to the maximum spell level they can cancel (4th vs. 8th) and burnout capacity (20 vs. 50 levels).
mechanics_evaluation.mastery_stone: Proficiency bonus increase by 1 is a very potent effect, rightly categorized as Legendary.
mechanics_evaluation.regeneration_stone: Regaining 15 HP per hour is a consistent, slow, but powerful regeneration effect appropriate for a Legendary item.
mechanics_evaluation.reserve_stone: The spell storage rules are detailed: stores up to 3 levels total, casting cost is based on the stored level, and it retains the original caster's stats. This is a complex but well-explained mechanic.
